method_summary: Introduces Moment-Video, a benchmark with 1,000 human-verified video-QA pairs across 7 domains and 25 subcategories, covering four task types: Temporal Occurrence, Temporal Counting, Action Description, and Temporal Reasoning.
key_innovation: Moment-Video benchmark specifically designed to diagnose temporal fidelity of video MLLMs through momentary visual event understanding, focusing on transient visual evidence rather than persistent objects or global context.
limitations_stated: Denser frame sampling improves some models but does not eliminate the bottleneck
Longer videos introduce stronger temporal-localization challenges
Current video MLLMs lack temporally faithful representations for capturing, preserving, and using brief but decisive visual evidence
title: Moment-Video: Diagnosing Temporal Fidelity of Video MLLMs on Momentary Visual Events
